name: Peanut-Crusted Chicken W/Thai Hot/Sweet Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Dry roasted peanuts, ground - fine
recipeInstructions:
1. Discard the outer leaves of the lemongrass and slice thin the bottom.
- Mash the sliced lemongrass to a paste in a mortar with a pestle.
2. In a shallow bowl, stir together the peanuts, cornmeal, 4 tablespoons garlic, gingerroot, lemongrass, black pepper, salt, and 1/3 cup flour.
- Have ready two separate shallow bowls containing the remaining flour and buttermilk.
- Dredge each chicken piece well in the flour, shaking off the excess, dip them in the buttermilk, letting the excess drip off, and roll them in the peanut mixture.
- Transfer the coated pieces to an oiled shallow baking dish.
3. Bake the chicken, covered tightly with foil, in the middle of a preheated 350°F oven for 45 minutes.
- Remove the foil carefully and bake the chicken for 20-25 more minutes, or until golden (the chicken will crisp as it cools).
4. In a small saucepan combine the vinegar, sugar, remaining 2 1/2 tablespoons garlic, crushed red chilies, nam pla, and water.
- Bring the mixture to a boil and simmer until reduced by half.
5. Serve the chicken with the sauce.
